title: monsonia salmoniflora
intro: Monsonia salmoniflora, also known as the "River Bushman's Poison" or "Bushman's Poison", is a plant species that belongs to the family Geraniaceae, native to southern Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Monsonia salmoniflora include its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help reduce pain and fever. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use by making a decoction from the roots, leaves, or stems, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
